messages.content: hello

Hello! I'm your iSupply AI Studio creative director, specializing in visual content creation with **OpenAI gpt-image-2** (currently active).

I can help you create:
- **Product photography** — hero shots, packshots, lifestyle scenes
- **Lifestyle imagery** — model + product campaigns
- **Brand content** — carousels, editorial, social media assets

What would you like to create today? Tell me about your product or campaign idea, and we'll build it together.
i want to replicate this

## Agent 9 — Style Replication Analysis

### Step 1: Full Visual Breakdown

---

#### 🔤 Typography

| Layer | Content | Position | Size | Font Style | Weight | Color |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | "MOBILE / *EDITING* / CLUB" | Top-center, stacked 3 lines | ~18pt (3% frame height) | Sans-serif + italic mix | Medium | White (hex #FFFFFF) |
| 2 | "How to use" | Center, above hero headline | ~38pt (6% frame height) | Serif, mixed weight | Light-medium | White (hex #FFFFFF) |
| 3 | "Claude." | Center-dominant, hero | ~180pt (28% frame height) | Bold sans-serif | Black/Heavy | White (hex #FFFFFF) |
| 4 | "*for Brands*" | Below hero headline, center | ~52pt (8% frame height) | Serif italic | Regular | White (hex #FFFFFF) |
| 5 | "OUR WORKFLOWS →" | Bottom-center, pill button | ~22pt | Sans-serif medium | Medium | Black (hex #1A1A1A) on white pill (hex #FFFFFF) |

**Typography hierarchy:** 3-tier system — eyebrow (MOBILE EDITING CLUB) → subhead (How to use) → hero (Claude.) → qualifier (*for Brands*) → CTA pill

---

#### 📐 Layout Grid

- **Subject:** Center-to-right, occupies ~65% of frame width, head in upper 40%, hands/ball in lower 60%
- **Product (basketball):** Lower-center, held at roughly 60% down the frame, occupies ~40% of frame width — dominant foreground
- **Negative space:** Upper-left quadrant — clear sky, breathing room
- **Text overlay:** Hero text sits over torso/mid-body — subject bleeds THROUGH the typography (model extends arm behind "Claude" headline)
- **CTA pill:** Bottom-center, ~15% from bottom edge

---

#### 🎨 Color Palette

| Element | Color | Hex |
|---|---|---|
| Sky background | Powder blue, slightly desaturated | hex #8BB5D4 |
| Skin tone | Warm medium olive | hex #C4956A (Fitzpatrick III–IV) |
| Jacket | Warm cream/sand | hex #D4C4A0 |
| Basketball | Burnt orange-brown | hex #C4622A |
| Text (all overlay) | Clean white | hex #FFFFFF |
| CTA pill | White with dark text | hex #FFFFFF / #1A1A1A |
| Overall grade | Cool-toned, slightly desaturated, lifted shadows — editorial clean |

---

#### 📸 Composition Geometry

- **Camera angle:** Low angle, approximately +15–20° looking UP at subject
- **Focal length:** Wide-to-normal — ~35mm equivalent, slight foreground distortion on basketball (appears enlarged)
- **Depth of field:** Medium-shallow — subject sharp, background softly blurred (outdoor court + road visible but defocused)
- **Rule of thirds:** Subject's face sits on upper-center intersection; ball anchors lower-center third
- **Dynamic energy:** Subject leans INTO camera — outstretched right arm breaks top-right frame edge, creating aggressive forward motion

---

#### 💡 Lighting

- **Direction:** Soft directional from above and slightly camera-right (~1–2 o'clock)
- **Quality:** Soft natural daylight — overcast or bright open shade, no hard shadows
- **Temperature:** Neutral-cool, approximately 5500–6000K
- **Shadows:** Very soft, minimal — almost shadowless on face
- **Atmosphere:** Clean outdoor, slight haze in distant background, no lens flare

---

#### 🎭 Overall Aesthetic

**Campaign/Bold** — high-energy editorial sports lifestyle. Magazine-grade composition with aggressive typography layering. Model breaks the text plane (arm extends past headline), creating 3D depth. The design treats text and subject as equal visual elements on the same layer.

---

### Step 3: What Do You Want to Replace?

Let's swap out the content while keeping this exact visual structure. Tell me your choices:

1. **Model** — keep this look (mid-20s male, olive skin, curly dark hair, cream jacket) or replace? If replace, describe your model.
2. **Product** — keep basketball or replace with your product? If replace, what is it?
3. **Text layers** — what should each say?
   - Eyebrow (top): currently "MOBILE EDITING CLUB"
   - Subhead: currently "How to use"
   - Hero headline: currently "Claude."
   - Qualifier: currently "*for Brands*"
   - CTA button: currently "OUR WORKFLOWS →"
4. **Setting** — keep outdoor basketball court with blue sky, or change?
5. **Color grade** — keep cool editorial, or adjust?
6. **Camera/composition** — keep low-angle dynamic, or modify?
replace everything, just the camera composition is the same.
Let's collect all your replacements at once:

follow_up.description: A woman in her 20s — I'll describe more details next
follow_up.label: Young woman, 20s

follow_up.description: A man in his 20s — I'll describe more details next
follow_up.label: Young man, 20s

follow_up.description: A woman in her 30s–40s — I'll describe more details next
follow_up.label: Older woman, 30s–40s

follow_up.description: A man in his 30s–40s — I'll describe more details next
follow_up.label: Older man, 30s–40s

Specifically:
- **Product name** (e.g. "iSupply Pro 2 earbuds")
- **Shape/form** (e.g. stem-style earbuds with charging case, matte white)
- **Key visual detail** (e.g. logo on the case, color, finish)

Perfect. Product identified.

**iSupply Pro 2 earbuds** — stem-style TWS earbuds in a rounded rectangular charging case, matte white finish, lid open showing both earbuds seated inside. Small oval sensor on each earbud stem, charging contacts on case exterior (right side). Form factor is nearly identical to AirPods Pro generation.

---

Now I have everything I need. Here's what I'm building:

**Confirmed brief:**
- **Model:** Chinese-Filipina, mid-20s
- **Product:** iSupply Pro 2 earbuds — matte white rounded case, lid open, both earbuds visible — held toward camera like the basketball in the reference
- **Setting:** Beach with powder blue sky
- **Eyebrow:** "iSupply Philippines"
- **Subhead:** "Active Noise Cancellation"
- **Hero headline:** "PRO 2"
- **Qualifier:** *(you left blank — I'll decide: "for your world")*
- **CTA:** "Shop now →"
- **Grade:** Cool editorial, like reference

Building the Master Prompt now...

---

## 🎬 Master Prompt

```
[API:openai,model=gpt-image-2,quality=high,size=1024x1536,format=png]

Beach setting, powder blue sky (hex #8BB5D4) slightly desaturated, soft white sand and 
shallow ocean visible in background, defocused at medium-shallow depth of field. 
Chinese-Filipina woman, mid-20s, Fitzpatrick III — golden beige complexion with warm 
yellow undertone, natural skin micro-texture visible, light catches the high planes of 
the cheekbones — not airbrushed, authentic skin with subtle pore visibility. Dark 
straight hair, wind-tousled. Wearing a light cream or sand-toned casual jacket over a 
white fitted top — relaxed, editorial beach styling. Low camera angle approximately 
+15 to +20 degrees looking up at subject, 35mm wide equivalent, slight foreground 
enlargement on product. Subject occupies center-to-right 65% of frame width, head in 
upper 40%, leaning dynamically INTO the camera with energy and forward momentum, one 
arm extending outward past the frame edge. She holds the iSupply Pro 2 wireless earbuds 
charging case — matte white rounded rectangular case, lid open revealing two white 
stem-style earbuds seated inside, small oval sensor visible on each stem — held 
forward toward camera at lower-center of frame, occupying approximately 38% of frame 
width, case dominating the foreground with slight wide-angle enlargement. 
Image 1 = iSupply Pro 2 earbuds reference — match the matte white rounded case shape, 
open lid, dual earbud seating, and white finish exactly. Not AirPods — iSupply Pro 2 
branding. Soft natural daylight from above and slightly camera-right, approximately 
5500K neutral-cool temperature, near-shadowless on face, clean overcast-bright quality. 
Cool-toned editorial color grade, slightly desaturated, lifted shadows, clean whites. 
Top-center text reading "iSupply Philippines" (i-S-u-p-p-l-y) stacked across 3 lines, 
white sans-serif medium weight, approximately 18pt. Center text reading 
"Active Noise Cancellation" in white sans-serif regular weight, approximately 38pt, 
positioned above the hero headline. Massive center-dominant hero headline reading 
"PRO 2" (P-R-O-2) in white bold sans-serif, approximately 180pt occupying 28% of 
frame height, overlapping the model's torso — model's arm and body bleed through and 
behind the text layer. Below the hero headline, italic serif text reading 
"for your world" in white, approximately 52pt. Bottom-center rounded pill button 
reading "Shop now →" in dark text on white pill background, approximately 22pt. 
Typography: Helvetica Neue or SF Pro Display style throughout. At least 25% negative 
space in upper-left quadrant — clear sky visible. Photorealism, sharp subject, 
medium-shallow depth of field, background beach softly blurred. 
No extra text, no duplicate letters, no hallucinated words, no watermarks.
```

---

## ⚙️ API Configuration
→ AUTO-FILLED by `[API:]` tag on line 1

| Param | Value |
|---|---|
| Model | gpt-image-2 |
| Quality | high |
| Size | 1024×1536 (2:3 portrait) |
| Format | PNG |
| Background | auto |
| Endpoint | `/v1/images/edits` (product ref attached) |

---

## 📋 Canvas Workflow

```
STEP 1 — Upload iSupply Pro 2 product photo → UploadNode
         Tags: isupply-pro-2, earbuds, matte-white, charging-case, open-lid, stem-style

STEP 2 — Paste Master Prompt → PromptNode
         Connect: UploadNode ──▶ PromptNode

STEP 3 — Generate → review result

STEP 4 — Seed Explorer in OutputNode for composition variants
```

---

Ready to generate? Upload your product photo to a **UploadNode** first, tag it `isupply-pro-2, earbuds, matte-white, charging-case`, connect it to a **PromptNode**, paste the Master Prompt, and fire. 

Want me to adjust anything — the qualifier text, the model styling, or the composition energy?

remove the word "for your world."
change the color grading with a bit of warm sand color
change the size to a 4:5 instagram post.

Done. Three changes applied:

1. **Qualifier line removed** — "for your world" stripped
2. **Color grade** — shifting from pure cool editorial to cool-with-warm-sand undertone (hex #C4A882 sand warm cast, slightly lifted)
3. **Size** — 4:5 → `1024×1280`

messages.content:
Canvas has the existing prompt node. Here's the updated Master Prompt — paste this into `promptNode-1778127998843-s3otvi` to replace the current one:

---

## 🎬 Master Prompt (Updated)

```
[API:openai,model=gpt-image-2,quality=high,size=1024x1280,format=png]

Beach setting, powder blue sky (hex #8BB5D4) slightly desaturated with warm sand 
undertones, soft white sand and shallow ocean visible in background, defocused at 
medium-shallow depth of field. Chinese-Filipina woman, mid-20s, Fitzpatrick III — 
golden beige complexion with warm yellow undertone, natural skin micro-texture visible, 
light catches the high planes of the cheekbones — not airbrushed, authentic skin with 
subtle pore visibility. Dark straight hair, wind-tousled. Wearing a light cream or 
sand-toned casual jacket over a white fitted top — relaxed, editorial beach styling. 
Low camera angle approximately +15 to +20 degrees looking up at subject, 35mm wide 
equivalent, slight foreground enlargement on product. Subject occupies center-to-right 
65% of frame width, head in upper 40%, leaning dynamically INTO the camera with energy 
and forward momentum, one arm extending outward past the frame edge. She holds the 
iSupply Pro 2 wireless earbuds charging case — matte white rounded rectangular case, 
lid open revealing two white stem-style earbuds seated inside, small oval sensor 
visible on each stem — held forward toward camera at lower-center of frame, occupying 
approximately 38% of frame width, case dominating the foreground with slight

Not AirPods — iSupply Pro 2. Soft natural daylight from above and slightly

undertone — hex #C4A882 sand warmth bleeding into midtones and shadows, lifted blacks,

positioned above the hero headline. Massive center-dominant hero headline reading 
"PRO 2" (P-R-O-2) in white bold sans-serif, approximately 180pt occupying 28% of

---

## ⚙️ API Configuration

|---|---|---|

| Color grade | Cool + warm sand `#C4A882` | ✅ Updated |
| Qualifier text | Removed | ✅ Done |
| Quality | `high` | unchanged |
| Format | `png` | unchanged |

Paste into the existing PromptNode, keep the UploadNode connection live, and generate. Ready when you are.
the color grading is still a bit off. look at this color grading style

Got it. This is a completely different color grade from what I had. Let me extract it precisely.

---

## Color Grade Analysis

**This image is:** Warm golden-hour sunset, Philippines tropical beach — the opposite of cool editorial.

| Element | Color | Hex |
|---|---|---|
| Sky (blown highlights) | Near-white warm cream | hex #FFF5E0 |
| Sky (mid zone) | Soft amber-gold | hex #F5C97A |
| Horizon glow | Deep warm amber | hex #E8A83C |
| Background water | Golden-bronze shimmer | hex #C4923A |
| Shadows (hair, background) | Warm deep brown, not black | hex #3D2810 |
| Skin highlights | Warm amber-bronze | hex #C4873A |
| Skin midtones | Deep caramel | hex #8B5E35 |
| Wooden deck | Warm tan-gold | hex #B89060 |
| Overall cast | **Warm amber-gold throughout — no cool tones anywhere** | |

**Grade characteristics:**
- **Highlights:** Blown warm white → cream → amber gradient
- **Midtones:** Pushed amber-orange, very warm
- **Shadows:** Warm deep brown — blacks lifted, never cold
- **Saturation:** Medium-high in warm tones only (oranges/yellows amplified)
- **Contrast:** Moderate — soft, not punchy
- **Color temperature:** Very warm, approximately 3000–3200K golden hour

**This is NOT the same vibe as the reference basketball image.** That was cool + desaturated. This is warm + amber-gold.

---

**Clarifying question before I update the prompt:**

Your original instruction was *"keep cool editorial like reference"* — but this new color grade is the opposite (warm golden hour). Do you want me to:

**A)** Replace the color grade entirely with this warm amber-golden hour style
**B)** Blend both — cool sky/upper frame + warm golden tones in lower frame and on skin
**C)** Use this warm grade but keep it slightly more restrained (not full blown-out sunset)

Which direction?
C

Perfect. **Restrained warm golden grade** — amber warmth in skin and midtones, controlled highlights (not blown out), cool sky stays in upper frame, blacks lifted but warm.

Here's the updated color grade block dropped into the full prompt:

---

## 🎬 Master Prompt (Updated — Color Grade v3)

```
[API:openai,model=gpt-image-2,quality=high,size=1024x1280,format=png]

Beach setting, powder blue sky (hex #8BB5D4) in upper frame transitioning to warm 
amber-gold in lower frame, soft white sand and shallow ocean visible in background, 
defocused at medium-shallow depth of field. Chinese-Filipina woman, mid-20s, 
Fitzpatrick III-IV — warm caramel skin with amber undertone, natural skin micro-texture 
visible, warm golden light catches the high planes of the cheekbones and brow ridge — 
not airbrushed, authentic skin with subtle pore visibility. Dark straight hair, 
wind-tousled. Wearing a light cream or sand-toned casual jacket over a white fitted 
top — relaxed, editorial beach styling. Low camera angle approximately +15 to +20 
degrees looking up at subject, 35mm wide equivalent, slight foreground enlargement on 
product. Subject occupies center-to-right 65% of frame width, head in upper 40%, 
leaning dynamically INTO the camera with energy and forward momentum, one arm extending 
outward past the frame edge. She holds the iSupply Pro 2 wireless earbuds charging case 
— matte white rounded rectangular case, lid open revealing two white stem-style earbuds 
seated inside, small oval sensor visible on each stem — held forward toward camera at 
lower-center of frame, occupying approximately 38% of frame width, case dominating the 
foreground with slight wide-angle enlargement. Image 1 = iSupply Pro 2 earbuds 
reference — match the matte white rounded case shape, open lid, dual earbud seating, 
and white finish exactly. Not AirPods — iSupply Pro 2. Soft natural directional 
sunlight from above and slightly camera-right at 15 degrees above horizon, 
approximately 3400K warm-neutral temperature, soft-edged shadows, golden specular 
highlights on skin and hair edges. Color grade: restrained warm golden — amber-gold 
warmth (hex #E8A83C) pushed into midtones and skin tones, highlights controlled and 
not blown out (cream-white ceiling hex #FFF0D0), shadows lifted and warm brown 
(hex #3D2810) never cold-black, cool powder blue preserved only in upper sky zone, 
medium saturation on warm tones only, soft contrast. Top-center text reading 
"iSupply Philippines" (i-S-u-p-p-l-y) stacked across 3 lines, white sans-serif medium 
weight, approximately 18pt. Center text reading "Active Noise Cancellation" in white 
sans-serif regular weight, approximately 38pt, positioned above the hero headline. 
Massive center-dominant hero headline reading "PRO 2" (P-R-O-2) in white bold 
sans-serif, approximately 180pt occupying 28% of frame height, overlapping the 
model's torso — model's arm and body bleed through and behind the text layer. 
Bottom-center rounded pill button reading "Shop now →" in dark text on white pill 
background, approximately 22pt. Typography: Helvetica Neue or SF Pro Display style 
throughout. At least 25% negative space in upper-left quadrant — clear sky visible. 
Photorealism, sharp subject, medium-shallow depth of field, background beach softly 
blurred. No extra text, no duplicate letters, no hallucinated words, no watermarks.
```

---

## ⚙️ What Changed

| Element | Before | Now |
|---|---|---|
| Color grade | Cool editorial + sand undertone | Restrained warm golden — amber midtones, controlled highlights, warm shadows |
| Skin tone | Fitzpatrick III golden beige | Fitzpatrick III–IV warm caramel + amber undertone |
| Lighting temp | 5500K neutral-cool | 3400K warm-neutral |
| Sky | Full cool blue | Cool blue upper frame only, transitions warm below |
| Highlights | Desaturated | Cream-white ceiling, not blown |
| Shadows | Lifted neutral | Lifted warm brown hex #3D2810 |

Paste into the PromptNode, keep the UploadNode connected, and generate. Want anything else tweaked before you fire?

next is create a few slides, every slides is a features of the airpods pro 2, are you able to browse web or can you make features? what is the best way these slides? lets brainstorm first. maybe you have idea.

Great question — let's brainstorm before building anything.

---

## The Core Challenge

You're making a **feature carousel** for iSupply Pro 2. Each slide = one feature. The reference layout (hero text overlaid on model + product, dynamic low-angle) is perfect for this — but we need to think about:

1. **What features to highlight**
2. **How each slide feels different without losing consistency**
3. **Whether to use the same model pose or vary it**

---

## On Browsing / Features

I can't browse the web, but I **don't need to** — based on the product photo you uploaded (AirPods Pro-gen form factor), the standard feature set for this tier of TWS earbuds is well-established. Here's what I'd confidently build slides around:

| # | Feature | Hero Headline | Subhead |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Active Noise Cancellation | **ANC** | Block the world out |
| 2 | Transparency Mode | **HEAR** | Stay aware, stay present |
| 3 | Battery Life | **30H** | All day. Then some. |
| 4 | Water Resistance | **IPX4** | Built for the beach |
| 5 | Spatial Audio | **SOUND** | Music all around you |
| 6 | Wireless Charging | **CHARGE** | Drop. Done. |

Each headline is **short, bold, punchy** — fits the "PRO 2" hero text style from the reference perfectly.

---

## Slide Consistency Options

Three directions we could go:

**Option A — Same model, same pose, different headline per slide**
Easiest visual consistency. Model barely changes. Only the text and subhead swap. Risk: feels repetitive.

**Option B — Same model, different interaction per slide**
Slide 1: holds case toward camera. Slide 2: earbud in ear, eyes closed. Slide 3: earbud in ear, mid-activity. More dynamic, tells a story. My recommendation.

**Option C — No model, product-only hero shots per slide**
Clean macro shots of the earbuds for each feature. Very different from slide 1 (which has model). Good for variety in a longer carousel.

---

## My Recommendation

**Slides 1–2:** Model-forward (like the reference — dynamic low angle, product held or worn)
**Slides 3–4:** Product close-up / macro (case, single earbud detail)
**Slides 5–6:** Model lifestyle (wearing earbuds, ambient scene, less text-heavy)

This gives the carousel **visual rhythm** — wide → macro → lifestyle — instead of 6 identical compositions.

---

## Questions Before I Build

1. **How many slides?** 3, 5, or 6?
2. **Which features?** Pick from my list above or tell me yours
3. **Model consistency** — same woman throughout, or mix model + product-only slides?
4. **Text style** — keep the same typography system (big hero word + subhead + CTA), or some slides go minimal/no text?

What feels right?

maybe 4 slides, those 4 top features. lets do your recommendation. but how are we able to have a model consistency and product consistency specially those in ear? do you need me to upload an image reference wearing it in ear? or what? text style must not be design just minimal and readable.

Really good question — this is the core challenge of AI carousels. Let me be straight with you:

---

## Model & Product Consistency — The Reality

### Model Consistency
GPT-Image-2 **cannot guarantee the same face** across separate generations. There's no seed lock like Gemini. Options:

**Option A — Upload a face reference photo**
You upload a photo of the model (or a person close to your vision) → I attach it as `Image 2` in every slide prompt → model instructs "preserve face from Image 2." Best consistency we can get with gpt-image-2.

**Option B — Very tight description**
Lock every detail — Fitzpatrick scale, specific facial features, hair — and keep it identical across all 4 prompts. Less reliable but works for general consistency (same *type* of person, not exact same face).

**Option C — Generate slide 1 first, save output, use it as reference for slides 2–4**
Once slide 1 generates a good model, feed that generated image back as a reference for the remaining slides. This is actually the **most practical workflow** for gpt-image-2 carousels.

→ **My recommendation: Option C.** Generate slide 1 first → use that output as model reference for slides 2, 3, 4.

---

### In-Ear Product Consistency
Yes — **upload a reference of the earbud worn in-ear** if you have one. Even a generic AirPods-in-ear photo works — I'll instruct the model to match the form factor. Without it, GPT-Image-2 tends to hallucinate earbud placement.

If you don't have one, I can describe it tightly enough for slides 2–3 and we iterate.

---

## Proposed 4-Slide Plan

| Slide | Feature | Composition | Text Style |
|---|---|---|---|
| **1** | Active Noise Cancellation | Low-angle dynamic, holds case toward camera (reference layout) | Minimal — just the feature word + one line |
| **2** | Battery Life — 30H | Same low angle, earbud worn in left ear, eyes forward, confident | Minimal — "30H" big, one subline |
| **3** | IPX4 Water Resistance | Product macro — case on wet sand, water droplets, beach context | Minimal — "IPX4" + one line, no model |
| **4** | Spatial Audio | Model wearing earbuds, eyes closed, serene — lifestyle feel | Minimal — "SOUND" or "360°" + one line |

---

## Revised Text System (Minimal)

Dropping the full typography stack from slide 1. Each slide gets:

```
[Small eyebrow — "iSupply Pro 2"]
[Hero word — e.g. "ANC" or "30H"]
[One subline — e.g. "Block the world out"]
[No CTA button on feature slides — or one consistent "Shop now →" on last slide only]
```

Clean. Readable. No design clutter.

---

## What I Need From You

1. **Do you have a face reference photo** for the model? (or use Option C — generate slide 1 first)
2. **Do you have an in-ear reference photo?** (or I describe it from scratch)
3. **Confirm the 4 features:** ANC · 30H Battery · IPX4 · Spatial Audio — good?
4. **Slide 3 — no model (product macro only)?** Or keep model in all 4?

Once you confirm, I'll build all 4 prompts ready to generate in sequence.

well i think we have to create a model first? then we will redo the first slide? what do you think? or we can use the model we created for the 1st slide? because i have created that already, the features is additional carousel. so total of 5 slides. 

 i have in ear reference photo

slide 3 is okay no model is fine.

Perfect thinking — yes, **reuse the model from slide 1** as the reference for slides 2, 4, 5. That's exactly Option C and the smartest workflow here.

Here's the plan:

---

## Revised 5-Slide Carousel Plan

| Slide | Feature | Composition | Model |
|---|---|---|---|
| **1** | Hero / PRO 2 intro | ✅ Already built | ✅ Already generated |
| **2** | Active Noise Cancellation | Low angle, model holds case toward camera (like slide 1 but ANC focus) | Output of slide 1 as reference |
| **3** | 30H Battery | Low angle, earbud worn in ear, confident forward gaze | Output of slide 1 + your in-ear reference |
| **4** | IPX4 Water Resistance | Product macro — case on wet sand, water droplets | No model |
| **5** | Spatial Audio | Model wearing earbuds, eyes closed, serene lifestyle | Output of slide 1 + your in-ear reference |

---

## Workflow
